pi_items.source: AirNav KOAK airport data, verified via official FAA airport information
Task specification requirement; verified as realistic wind direction for training scenario
Task specification requirement; 12 knots is realistic operating wind speed

AirNav runway heading data: Runway 30 is at magnetic heading 296° (~300°), which is 14° from 310° wind direction - closest match. Alternatives (28L/28R at 278°) are 32° away. Verified runway alignment analysis.

AirNav runway heading data: Both 28R and 28L are at 278° magnetic heading, which is 32° from 310° wind. Less ideal than runway 30 but acceptable. Runway 33 at 330° (20° difference) could also be reasonable but is conventionally a taxiway/alternative.
AirNav official data: Runway 12/30 is exactly 10,520 feet long
Reasonable tolerance for runway measurement verification; standard practice for runway data with potential survey variations
AirNav official data: KOAK has four runways (12/30, 10R/28L, 10L/28R, 15/33). Metadata min of 3 is satisfied.
AirNav official data: KOAK has exactly 4 active runways
Task specification in task.json and shell scripts setup_task.sh and export_result.sh

additional_findings.verifier_bug: verifier.py line 99 claims runway 28R is ~6,213 feet, but AirNav shows 28R (10L) is actually 5,457 feet. Runway 28L (10R) is 6,213 feet. This discrepancy could cause incorrect scoring if an agent selects 28R.

privileged_info_summary: KOAK (Oakland San Francisco Bay Airport) has four active runways. For a 310° wind direction, runway 30 at magnetic heading 296° is the optimal choice (14° difference), significantly better aligned than the acceptable alternatives 28L/28R at 278° (32° difference). Runway 30 is 10,520 feet long. The task correctly specifies these real-world airport parameters based on official FAA/AirNav data.
